title: state company block
purpose: H1.6 defines state company block for the agent-driven expert system. Persistence — journal, state.json, graphs, evidence.
scope: Owns `H1.6` only; siblings under `H1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §10 — Branch H — Persistence & state plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#10-branch-h-persistence-state-plane).
behavior_steps: At H1 program instantiation or pack selection, the conductor writes pack_id, active_role, template-pack version, and workstream lease refs into state.json company block—mirroring the journal Company section on dual-write.

When [B5.1](B5.1-active-role-from-template-pack.md) signals a role switch mid-goal, the conductor records the decision in journal before updating active_role; workers spawned thereafter inherit permissions from [B5.2](B5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md) pack schema.
Program-mode pursuits load integration manifest and artifact graph pointers from the company block so [C4](C4-index.md) orchestration reads consistent cross-stream contracts.
pack_id null preserves default repo harness pipelines; non-null pack_id overrides pipeline_id and verify suites per [F1](F1-index.md) without mixing pack fragments.
If company block drifts from journal Company on dual-write, or manifest refs point to stale nodes, preflight halts at H2 until conductor reconcile restores consistency.

Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `H1.6` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `H1.6` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `H1.6`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `H1.6` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.
Create or extend S0 script if behavior is file-derived.
Add unit test under tests/unit/test_h1_6.py when script exists.
Validate `H1.6` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `H1.6` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `H1.6`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: H1.6 defines state company block for the agent-driven expert system. Persistence — journal, state.json, graphs, evidence. In practice, the system at h1 program instantiation or pack selection, the conductor writes pack_id, active_role, template-pack version, and workstream lease refs into state.json company block—mirroring the journal company section on dual-write, then when [b5.1](b5.1-active-role-from-template-pack.md) signals a role switch mid-goal, the conductor records the decision in journal before updating active_role; workers spawned thereafter inherit permissions from [b5.2](b5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md) pack schema.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-h 2026-06-28 -->

The company block binds pack_id, active_role, template-pack version, and multi-goal workstream leases for company autopilot. Role switches mid-goal require journal-recorded conductor decision ([B5.1](B5.1-active-role-from-template-pack.md)); workers inherit role permissions from pack schema ([B5.2](B5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md)).

Integration manifest and artifact graph refs live here for program mode ([C4](C4-index.md)). pack_id null means default repo harness; instantiated packs override pipeline_id and verify suites per [F1](F1-index.md). Company block must stay consistent with journal Company section on dual-write.
